name: Fontanar from Azuqueca de Henares — circular by Guadalajara and the Camino del Cid
name_original: Fontanar desde Azuqueca de Henares — circular por Guadalajara y el Camino del Cid
region_intro: Touring cycling around Azuqueca De Henares, located in the province of Guadalajara, is characterized by its diverse natural features, including the Henares River and its riparian forest. The landscape offers a mix of urban green spaces like Quebradilla Urban Park and dedicated cycling routes, many of which utilize mostly paved surfaces. The region also features the Ornithological Reserve of Azuqueca, providing unique opportunities for wildlife observation during rides. These varied terrains make Azuqueca de Henares suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Azuqueca De Henares The most popular touring cycling route is Fontanar from Azuqueca de Henares — circular by Guadalajara and the Camino del Cid, a 35.2 miles (56.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through Guadalajara and along the historic Camino del Cid. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Parish Church of Quer – Sotolargo Trail loop from Azuqueca, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This route leads past the charming clock tower of the Church of Santa Eulalia de Mérida and the Hermitage of La Soledad in Quer. Local touring cyclists also love the Access to the Henares Riverside – Cave of the Gigantones loop from Azuqueca, a 28.2 miles (45.3 km) trail leading through the Henares riverside, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Azuqueca De Henares is defined by the tranquil Henares River, its riparian forests, and connections to historical landmarks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Azuqueca De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Azuqueca De Henares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Azuqueca de Henares?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Azuqueca de Henares, catering to various skill levels. These include 5 easy routes, 14 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Azuqueca de Henares?
A: Yes, Azuqueca de Henares offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. Many routes utilize mostly paved surfaces and paths, providing a comfortable and safe experience. You can find 5 easy routes in the area.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Azuqueca de Henares are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189711599/fontanar-from-azuqueca-de-henares-circular-by-guadalajara-and-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanar from Azuqueca de Henares — circular by Guadalajara and the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Azuqueca de Henares?
A: The terrain around Azuqueca de Henares is diverse, featuring a mix of urban green spaces, riparian forests along the Henares River, and connections to nearby towns. Many routes utilize mostly paved surfaces and dedicated bike lanes, though some more challenging rides may include unpaved or difficult sections.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while cycling around Azuqueca de Henares?
A: While cycling, you can explore the tranquil Henares River and its riparian forest, which offers scenic beauty and a rich habitat. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21772177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Access to the Henares Riverside – Cave of the Gigantones loop from Azuqueca&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to experience the riverside. Additionally, the Ornithological Reserve of Azuqueca is a significant natural attraction, serving as a sanctuary for over 200 bird species.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks to visit on a touring cycling route?
A: Absolutely. Cycling routes often incorporate nearby towns and historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38248495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Quer – Sotolargo Trail loop from Azuqueca&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the charming clock tower of the Church of Santa Eulalia de Mérida and the Hermitage of La Soledad in Quer. Within Azuqueca itself, you can find the San Miguel Parish Church and the Hermitage of La Soledad. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3405880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Infantado&lt;/a&gt; in the wider region.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes offer panoramic views of the area. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalba Route – Mirador de la Peñalba loop from Azuqueca&lt;/a&gt; leads towards Mirador de la Peñalba, providing stunning vistas over the town and the valley, surrounded by pine forests.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter on the touring cycling routes?
A: The Henares River and its riparian forest provide a habitat for various flora and fauna. The Ornithological Reserve of Azuqueca is particularly notable, being a sanctuary for over 200 bird species. Cyclists can enjoy nature observation, especially along the riverside paths.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about cycling in Azuqueca de Henares?
A: The touring cycling routes in Azuqueca de Henares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse natural features, including the tranquil Henares River, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels, from easy rides to more challenging explorations of historical landmarks.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes in Azuqueca de Henares?
A: Azuqueca de Henares is well-equipped for cyclists, and as a town, it offers various parking options. Many routes start directly from the town, making it convenient to park your vehicle and begin your ride. Specific parking details may vary by route starting point, but general municipal parking is available.

Q: Is Azuqueca de Henares accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: Azuqueca de Henares has good public transport connections, including train services, which can be convenient for cyclists. It's advisable to check the specific public transport operator's rules regarding bicycles, especially during peak hours, to plan your journey effectively.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Azuqueca de Henares?
A: Azuqueca de Henares offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling. Summer can be warm, but early morning or late afternoon rides are still enjoyable, especially along the shaded riparian areas of the Henares River. Winter cycling is also an option, with milder temperatures compared to more northern regions.
tips: Fontarar in a small town near the Henares River north of Guadalajara. Good place to stop to rest in the Plaza Mayor and the fresh water fountain.

The city of Guadalajara was founded as an outpost by Arabs in the 8th century. Due to its good strategic location, it later developed into a solid defensive point and grew in size. It is said that they were founded later in 1085 by the Spaniard Alvar Fáñez under King Alfonso VI. was taken. Alvar Fáñez was a follower of the Castilian knight El Cid.

The Palacio del Infantado is a spectacular and striking Gothic Renaissance palace from the late 15th century. Although the building was often renovated and even partially destroyed during the civil war, it still has the charm it had five hundred years ago.
